title: Daily Market Digest: March 31, 2026
subtitle: Geopolitical Tensions and Oil Prices Weigh on Markets; Fed Maintains Dovish Stance

items.headline: U.S. Futures Mixed Amid Iran Conflict Concerns, Fed Signals No Immediate Rate Hikes
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed mixed movements early Tuesday, March 31, 2026, as investors continued to grapple with the escalating conflict in Iran and its impact on oil prices. While major indices ended Monday's session mixed, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reiterated that inflation appears under control and there's no immediate need to raise interest rates. The S&P 500 is nearing correction territory, reflecting a cautious overall market sentiment. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Down 0.39% on Monday. The index is nearing correction territory, down 9.4% from its January peak.
Declined 0.73% on Monday. The index is off 13.4% from its highs.
Rose 0.11% on Monday. The index is off 10.5% from its highs.

sections.title: Top Movers (As of March 30, 2026, Close or Week-Ending)

items.headline: Compass Diversified Holdings Rises on Acquisition News
items.summary: Shares of Compass Diversified Holdings ($CODI) jumped 15% on Monday after the company announced it would sell the food service business of its majority-owned subsidiary, SternoCandleLamp Holdings, to Archer Foodservice Partners.

items.headline: Brown-Forman Among Weekly Gainers
items.summary: Brown-Forman ($BF-B) saw a significant gain over the past week, up 16.5% as of Monday's close, reflecting broader market movements.

items.headline: Sysco Falls on Major Acquisition Deal
items.summary: Food distributor Sysco ($SYY) shares fell 15.1% in the past week, with Monday's trading showing continued pressure, following its announcement of a $29 billion deal to acquire supplier Jetro Restaurant Depot.

items.headline: Micron Technology Leads Weekly Decline
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) experienced a substantial drop of 20.4% over the past five trading days, making it one of the week's biggest losers.

items.headline: Energy and Defensive Sectors Lead Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty, Tech and Financials Lag
items.summary: Sector analysis for March 2026 shows Energy, Utilities, Materials, Industrials, and Consumer Staples outperforming, driven by safe-haven flows and rising commodity prices due to the Middle East conflict. Conversely, Technology, Communication Services, Consumer Discretionary, and Financials have lagged, with large-cap tech facing a 'malaise' and AI disruption fears.

items.headline: Fed Chair Powell Monitors Inflation Amid Energy Spikes, No Immediate Rate Hikes Planned
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated on Monday that the Fed is closely monitoring inflation and energy price spikes stemming from the Iran conflict. He indicated that while energy shocks tend to be temporary, a series of them could be concerning. Despite these concerns, Powell affirmed that inflation appears under control, and there is no immediate need to raise interest rates, providing some relief to investors.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Expected Today: Consumer Confidence and JOLTS Job Openings
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting two significant economic data releases today, March 31, 2026: March's consumer confidence index and February's JOLTS (Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey) job openings data. These reports will offer fresh insights into consumer sentiment and the health of the labor market, which are crucial for assessing the economic outlook.

items.headline: Investcorp Reports Q4 Loss; McCormick, TD Synnex, Nike Earnings Anticipated Today
items.summary: Investcorp Credit Management BDC, Inc. ($ICMB) reported a fourth-quarter loss of $9.4 million, or 65 cents per share, on Monday. Meanwhile, major companies including McCormick & Company Inc. ($MKC), TD Synnex Corp. ($SNX), and Nike Inc. ($NKE) are expected to release their earnings reports or hold annual general meetings today, March 31, 2026, which could impact market sentiment. TuHURA Biosciences ($HURA) also has an earnings report due.

items.headline: Iran Conflict Escalates, Driving Oil Prices Higher and Fueling Market Uncertainty
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in Iran continues to be a primary driver of market uncertainty, particularly impacting energy markets. Reports of drone strikes on oil tankers and Iran's assertion of control over the Strait of Hormuz have sent Brent crude prices to around $113 a barrel, and U.S. benchmark crude near $103, marking a surge of over 40% since the conflict began. The escalating tensions are causing tremors through global financial systems and raising concerns about inflation and economic growth.
